title: Course Module - Terminal & Command Line 💻
desc:
# Terminal Masterclass 🚀

The Terminal (Command Line Interface - CLI) is one of the most powerful tools for developers. It allows you to control your computer, run programs, manage files, use Git, deploy projects, and automate tasks using text commands instead of a graphical interface.

In this module, you will learn how to use the terminal like a professional developer.

---

# 📚 What You Will Learn

## 1. Introduction to Terminal

Understand what the terminal is and why developers use it.

### Topics Covered:
- What is Terminal?
- CLI vs GUI
- Why Developers Use Terminal
- Basic Structure of Commands

---

# 📁 File & Directory Management

Learn how to manage files and folders using commands.

### Commands:

```bash
ls        # List files
pwd       # Show current directory
cd        # Change directory
mkdir     # Create folder
rm        # Remove file/folder
```

### Example:

```bash
mkdir project
cd project
```

---

# 📄 File Operations

Work with files directly from terminal.

### Commands:

```bash
touch file.txt     # Create file
cp file1 file2     # Copy file
mv file1 file2     # Move/Rename file
rm file.txt        # Delete file
```

---

# ⚡ System Information

Check system details using terminal.

### Commands:

```bash
whoami      # Current user
uname -a    # System info
top         # Running processes
```

---

# 🧠 Package Management

Install and manage software using terminal.

### Linux Example:

```bash
apt update
apt install git
```

### Node.js Example:

```bash
npm install express
```

---

# 🌐 Git & GitHub with Terminal

Terminal is widely used for Git operations.

### Commands:

```bash
git init
git add .
git commit -m "message"
git push origin main
```

---

# 🔐 Permissions & Security

Manage file permissions in Linux systems.

### Commands:

```bash
chmod +x file.sh
chown user file.txt
```

---

# ⚙️ Process Management

Control running programs and processes.

### Commands:

```bash
ps
kill 1234
htop
```

---

# 🚀 Networking Commands

Check network and internet status.

### Commands:

```bash
ping google.com
curl https://api.example.com
ifconfig
```

---

# 🛠️ Automation with Terminal

Terminal can automate tasks using scripts.

### Example Script:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
echo "Hello World"
```

---

# 📦 Node.js & Project Running

Run development servers using terminal.

### Commands:

```bash
node app.js
npm start
npm run dev
```

---

# ☁️ Deployment with Terminal

Deploy projects using CLI tools.

### Platforms:
- Vercel CLI
- Netlify CLI
- Firebase CLI

### Example:

```bash
vercel deploy
```

---

# 💡 Professional Tips

## Best Practices:
- Learn shortcuts
- Use tab auto-complete
- Practice daily commands
- Keep terminal clean
- Use Git from terminal
- Learn Linux basics

---

# 📈 Career Importance

Terminal skills help you become:

- Full Stack Developer
- DevOps Engineer
- Backend Developer
- System Administrator
- Cloud Engineer

---

# 🚀 Why Terminal Is Important

✅ Faster workflow
✅ Full system control
✅ Essential for developers
✅ Used in servers & cloud
✅ Automation power

---

# 🎯 Final Motivation

The terminal is one of the most powerful tools in programming. Once you master it, your productivity and development speed increase dramatically.

> "Real developers are comfortable in the terminal." 💻🔥
